claim: VERIFIED: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements comprehensive harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents and blocks PRD readiness on project mutation or insufficient evidence.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates SHA256 digests of all project files, excluding allowed artifact roots
evidence.why: Core boundary audit mechanism that detects file mutations via cryptographic hashing

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files
evidence.why: Explicit change detection with change type (added/modified/deleted) for each violated file

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot captured before agent execution: buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir])
evidence.why: Runtime baseline capture for before/after comparison

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot captured after agent execution and compared: diffBoundarySnapshots(boundaryBefore, boundaryAfter)
evidence.why: Runtime change detection completes the audit cycle

evidence.summary: result.boundary includes project_mutation status ('violated' or 'clean'), allowed_write_roots, and changes array
evidence.why: Explicit boundary violation reporting structure in dispatch results

evidence.summary: Boundary blockers with code BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION added to readiness when changes detected
evidence.why: Direct integration between boundary audit and PRD readiness blocking

evidence.summary: Final readiness with boundary blockers sets prd_ready=false and status='blocked'
evidence.why: Proves that boundary violations prevent PRD readiness completion

evidence.summary: CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS includes Read, Write, Edit, Bash, WebFetch, WebSearch, Agent, and more
evidence.why: Proves full-tool Claude agents are explicitly supported with comprehensive tool access

evidence.summary: execution_policy specifies project_mutation: 'forbidden' and allowed_write_roots: [artifact output dir]
evidence.why: Explicit policy declaration that project mutation is forbidden, only artifact writes allowed

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rule: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files. If verification would require mutation, report it in missing.'
evidence.why: Agents are explicitly instructed to avoid project mutations during evidence gathering

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross_checker, and verifier roles with writes_code: false
evidence.why: Evidence agents are declaratively read-only by protocol definition

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues() creates blockers from agent error codes, missing evidence, or blocked recommendations
evidence.why: Agent problems (missing data, provider failures, blocked verdicts) become readiness blockers

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() creates blockers when project-scoped evidence lacks repo-relative path or only has external/user/unknown scope
evidence.why: Enforces that existing-project facts require concrete project evidence, not just external research

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks for required evidence agent roles (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) and blocks if incomplete or failed
evidence.why: Integration point where evidence agent results affect PRD readiness

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() for Claude passes --permission-mode, --settings, --tools, --allowedTools including Bash, Write, WebFetch, WebSearch, Agent
evidence.why: Shows the full Claude tool configuration in provider invocation, proving full-tool support

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' proves boundary audit catches writes and blocks readiness
evidence.why: Executable test demonstrating boundary violation detection and blocking

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' proves git file system integration for change detection
evidence.why: Shows boundary audit works with both git and non-git file tracking

evidence.summary: Test 'claude dispatch keeps tools available and relies on boundary enforcement' validates WebFetch, WebSearch, Write, Bash are allowed
evidence.why: Test proves full-tool Claude agents are used, not sandboxed/restricted tools

evidence.summary: Doctrine states dispatch '会在运行前后审计项目文件边界，任何越界改动都会变成 readiness blocker'
evidence.why: Primary product requirement doc confirming boundary audit is intentional design, not implementation detail

evidence.summary: Doctrine specifies evidence scope rules and that existing-project facts without project-scoped evidence must be blocked
evidence.why: Product-level requirement for evidence quality enforcement

risks: If boundary snapshot fails silently or has false negatives, agents could mutate projects without detection
If git file tracking is unavailable and walkFiles() has permission issues, boundary audit could be incomplete
If allowed_write_roots configuration is mis-specified, legitimate artifact writes could be flagged as violations

result.notes: Independent verification confirms all explorer claims: boundary audit is comprehensive (SHA256 hashing, git/walk fallback, explicit change detection), PRD blocking is direct (BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers prevent readiness), full-tool Claude agents are supported (CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS array, provider-adapter passes all tools), and evidence quality is enforced (projectEvidenceBlockingIssues, protocol-level writes_code: false). All line numbers match, all code paths exist, and integration points are verified.
